name: Push-up
description: Hands under shoulders, body in one straight line — lower until elbows reach about 90°, then press up.

name: Diamond Push-up
description: Index fingers and thumbs touching under the chest; keep elbows tucked to load the triceps.

name: Dip
description: Lean slightly forward, lower until your shoulders drop below your elbows, then press to a full lockout.

name: Pike Push-up
description: Hips high in an inverted V; lower the crown of your head toward the floor between your hands.

name: Pseudo Planche Push-up
description: Lean your shoulders out past your hands, fingers pointing back, to load the planche line.

name: Pull-up
description: From a dead hang, pull until your chin clears the bar; control the descent all the way down.

name: Chin-up
description: Underhand grip — drive your elbows down to your ribs and squeeze at the top.

name: Australian Row
description: Body straight under a low bar; pull your chest to the bar and squeeze the shoulder blades together.

name: Scapular Pull
description: Hanging with straight arms, pull your shoulder blades down and back without bending the elbows.

name: Pistol Squat
description: Sit back on one leg with the other extended forward; descend slowly and stand to full lockout.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
description: Rear foot elevated; drop straight down over the front heel, knee tracking over the toes.

name: Nordic Curl
description: Knees anchored, lower your torso as slowly as you can, resisting the whole way with the hamstrings.

name: Calf Raise
description: Rise onto the balls of your feet for a full stretch-to-squeeze; pause at the top of each rep.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
description: From a hang, raise straight legs to at least hip height without swinging or kipping.

name: Plank
description: Forearms down, body in one line from head to heels; brace your abs and squeeze your glutes.

name: Hollow Hold
description: Press your lower back flat into the floor with arms and legs extended just above it.

name: L-sit
description: Support on your hands, legs straight out in front and toes pointed — hold the compression.

name: Easy Run
description: Keep a conversational pace — you should be able to talk in full sentences the whole way.

name: Intervals
description: Alternate hard efforts with easy recovery; keep the hard reps fast but repeatable.
